LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. FKA CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND ANSWER. DEFENDANTS' (RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. FKA CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC. AKA CALIFORNIA-QUARTZ) MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER IS GRANTED. COURTS LIBERALLY ALLOW AMENDMENTS TO ANSWERS. (HULSEY V. KOEHLER (1990) 218 CAL.APP.3D 1150, 1159 ["IN PARTICULAR, LIBERALITY SHOULD BE DISPLAYED IN ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO ANSWERS, FOR A DEFENDANT DENIED LEAVE TO AMEND IS PERMANENTLY DEPRIVED OF A DEFENSE."].) DEFENSE COUNSEL RECENTLY DISCOVERED THAT RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. AND CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC. ARE DISTINCT ENTITIES. (GUAN DECL., EXS. 1 AND 2.) THEREFORE, IT IS APPROPRIATE FOR THEM TO FILE DISTINCT ANSWERS. THERE IS NO PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF'S CONCERN THAT CALIFORNIA-QUARTZ WILL "DISAPPEAR" LACKS MERIT SINCE IT HAS IN FACT FILED ITS OWN ANSWER ON MARCH 20, 2025. RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. HAS LEAVE TO FILE THE PROPOSED AMENDED ANSWER, WHICH REMOVES REFERENCE TO THE SEPARATE ENTITY AND INAPPOSITE ASBESTOS-RELATED STATUTES. THE COURT EXPRESSES NO OPINION REGARDING THE ANSWER FILED ON MARCH 20, 2025, THAT ONLY REFERENCES "CALIFORNIA QUARTZ" AND NOT CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES/COSTS IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Answer
Off Calendar Apr-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Julie Maneker, Esq. 562-437-4499 Metger Law Group for Plaintiff.
Michael Guan, Esq. (415) 766-3200 HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG LLP for Defendant Defendant RAPHAEL STONE.

ARGUED. Ms. Maneker contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments. The Court shares its comments. Mr. Guan responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. FKA CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC.'s Motion For Leave To Amend Answer.

Defendants' (RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. FKA CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC. AKA CALIFORNIA-QUARTZ) motion for leave to file an amended answer is granted.

Courts liberally allow amendments to answers. (Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159 ["In particular, liberality should be displayed in allowing amendments to answers, for a defendant denied leave to amend is permanently deprived of a defense."].) Defense counsel recently discovered that RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. and CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC. are distinct entities. (Guan Decl., Exs. 1 and 2.) Therefore, it is appropriate for them to file distinct answers. There is no prejudice to Plaintiff. Plaintiff's concern that CALIFORNIA-QUARTZ will "disappear" lacks merit since it has in fact filed its own answer on March 20, 2025. RAPHAEL STONE CA, INC. has leave to file the proposed amended answer, which removes reference to the separate entity and inapposite asbestos-related statutes. The court expresses no opinion regarding the answer filed on March 20, 2025, that only references "CALIFORNIA QUARTZ" and not CALIFORNIA QUARTZ, INC.

Plaintiff's request for fees/costs is denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
